architecture.summary: Tango jointly trains an LLM generator and an LLM verifier via interleaved RL. The generator produces multi-step reasoning trajectories and receives supervision from two reward sources: rule-based outcome-level rewards (correctness of final answer) and step-level rewards sampled from the verifier. The verifier generates natural-language verification responses containing step-wise judgments and final correctness labels, trained via RL on outcome-level rewards (verification accuracy + format compliance) without process-level annotations. Generator and verifier alternate training cycles, with the verifier updating once per three generator steps. Information flows bidirectionally: the generator's evolving reasoning trajectories provide training signal and distribution shifts that guide verifier improvement, while the verifier's stochastic step-level feedback guides generator exploration.
trained_components: class-aware reweighting scheme for verifier advantage computation
exponential decay schedule for step-level reward weighting

biblio.title: RL Tango: Reinforcing Generator and Verifier Together for Language Reasoning

claims.dataset: five competition-level math benchmarks (AIME 2025, AIME 2024, AMC 2023, MATH-500, OlympiadBench)
claims.statement: Tango with GRPO achieves 25.5% average relative improvement on five competition-level math benchmarks compared to vanilla RL methods trained only on outcome-level rewards

claims.dataset: four out-of-domain reasoning tasks (BoardgameQA, CRUXEval, StrategyQA, TableBench)
claims.statement: Tango with GRPO achieves 7.3% average relative improvement on four out-of-domain reasoning tasks compared to vanilla GRPO
Tango with GRPO doubles the accuracy on AIME 2025 (100% relative gain) compared to vanilla GRPO
Tango achieves 3.3× improvement in training efficiency: Tango matches vanilla GRPO's accuracy after 200 steps in only 60 generator steps
Tango's verifier achieves state-of-the-art step-level verification performance on ProcessBench among 7B/8B-scale models without using process-level labels, with 43.9% average F1 score
Among 7B/8B-scale reasoning LLMs, Tango-Qwen-7B achieves state-of-the-art performance averaging 49.5% accuracy on math tasks and 62.8% on out-of-domain tasks

The verifier progressively learns accurate step-level judgments by refining its chain-of-thought verification reasoning through RL training, gradually aligning step-level feedback with final correctness outcomes as the generator's reasoning trajectories evolve, without requiring explicit process-level annotations

claims.dataset: algorithmic reasoning task (last letter concatenation problem)
claims.statement: When the verifier is fixed (frozen), the generator's performance lags behind the co-evolving baseline, and when the generator is fixed, the verifier's progress quickly plateaus, demonstrating the necessity of bidirectional co-evolution for both components to achieve optimal performance
